sourceMovie.title: War of the Worlds
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto War of the Worlds (2005) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If War of the Worlds clicked for you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If War of the Worlds clicked for you, World War Z (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after War of the Worlds?
faq.answer: Try Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials, World War Z, 10 Cloverfield Lane—each shares DNA with War of the Worlds while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is War of the Worlds?
faq.answer: War of the Worlds (2005) is best described as Adventure, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is War of the Worlds worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
